reason: While this passage from Exodus 12 clearly emphasizes that the people are called to follow God's commandments and obey the instructions given through Moses and Aaron (וַיַּעֲשׂוּ כָּל־ בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל - 'all the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded'), there is no explicit instruction for the people to 'shut up' or remain silent. The text focuses on active obedience and movement (קוּמוּ צְּאוּ - 'rise up and depart'), not on silencing or refraining from speech. The narrative emphasizes doing and following God's ordinances, but does not contain a directive to be quiet.
